rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when adding annex content; avoid changing features, subcomponents, connections, or properties.
Use owner-appropriate scope: component type contracts belong in type scope, while implementation-level bindings belong only in implementation scope.
Do not introduce references that are not visible in the chosen owner scope, especially implementation-only paths from a broader scope.
generic_rules: Attach generated content to the correct owning declaration and keep surrounding structure unchanged.
Maintain valid scope boundaries between type-level interface content and implementation-level detail.
Prefer minimal edits that add the requested annex content without refactoring unrelated model text.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scoped AGREE content should appear in an implementation owner, typically after relevant structural declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not move or rewrite unrelated architectural elements while inserting annex content.
Do not place implementation-specific references into a broader owner scope where they are not visible.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names treated as AGREE variables
requirement-level threshold semantics beyond scope/owner preservation
